description:
Advances: specled.implementation_tier

Follow-up from specled_-q59.9.

## Repro

Enable the implementation tier in the orchestrator and run \`mix spec.check\` on this repo:

    enabled = SpecLedEx.Realization.Orchestrator.default_tiers() ++ [:implementation]
    SpecLedEx.Realization.Orchestrator.run(index, enabled_tiers: enabled)

Observe: \`(FunctionClauseError) no function clause matching in Macro.do_traverse_args/4\` from \`SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical.alpha_rename/1\` via \`Implementation.resolve_mfa_asts/4\`.

## Root cause

\`Binding.resolve/2\` returns AST in an api_boundary-specific wrapper shape:

    # lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex:~200
    defp clauses_ast(fun, arity, clauses) do
      {fun, arity, Enum.map(clauses, fn {_meta, args, guards, body} -> {args, guards, body} end)}
    end

The shape \`{fun, integer, [{args, guards, body}, ...]}\` is NOT a valid Elixir AST — Elixir AST is \`{name, meta, args}\` where meta is a keyword list and args is either a list of AST or a context atom. ApiBoundary.hash's \`canonicalize_head/1\` pattern-matches this shape explicitly and handles it.

But \`Implementation.resolve_mfa_asts/4\` forwards the same value to \`Canonical.normalize/1\`, which uses \`Macro.prewalk/3\`. Macro.prewalk sees the integer in meta position and crashes.

## Why it did not surface before q59.9

\`Binding.resolve\` had a destructure bug (fixed in specled_-q59 prep commit 7d11872) that made it silently return \`:error\` on every successful beam lookup. The impl tier's existing test suite either uses source-fallback paths (which return standard \`def\` AST nodes) OR compile ASTs from source that happen to bypass the issue. With the resolver bug fixed, real binding resolution now returns the non-standard wrapper and the impl tier crashes.

## Fix options

1. Reshape \`Binding.resolve\` to return standard \`{:def, meta, [{name, meta, args}, guards, body]}\` AST nodes. ApiBoundary's canonicalize_head would need a matching rewrite. This is the \"correct\" fix but touches multiple tier internals.

2. In \`Implementation.resolve_mfa_asts\`, unwrap the \`{fun, arity, clauses}\` shape and rebuild standard AST nodes before handing to Canonical.normalize. Localizes the fix to impl tier.

3. Teach \`Canonical.normalize\` to recognize and pass-through the api_boundary wrapper shape. Feels wrong — Canonical is tier-agnostic.

Option (2) is probably the cleanest localized fix.

## Scope

- \`lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex\` (most likely), OR
- \`lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex\` + callers (if taking the deeper fix)
- Test: enable \`:implementation\` in Orchestrator.default_tiers (or just run dogfood with it on) and assert no crash; at least one impl-tier drift/dangling finding surfaces correctly.

## Unblocks

Once this lands, \`Orchestrator.default_tiers/0\` can include \`:implementation\`, and \`specled.branch_guard.tier_dispatch_impl_opt_in\` becomes deletable.
title: [BUG] implementation tier crashes on Binding.resolve output shape
